---
title: "Tidy Data Evaluation"
author: "Patrick Sherry"
date: "2026-02-26"
format: html
---

## Introduction
#This dataset is from an ADP-Glo assay used to test kinase activity in my enzyme of study. 
#Tidy data requires that each variable be stored in its own column, each observation be stored in its own row, and each value occupy a single cell. The goal of this analysis is to determine whether the exported assay data meets those criteria.

## Data

```{r}
library(here)
library(readr)

my_data <- read_csv(here("data", "RAW", "RAW_DATA_ADPGLO_806.csv"))

head(my_data)
```
##Analysis 
#The dataset is mostly clean and readable. It is not fully tidy because replicates are stored in separate columns instead of one Replicate column. It also does not contain primary/foreign keys but it does have identifiers.
#In the table, Condition is the primary key. There are no foreign keys because there is only one table.
